It is a high-performance data structure server that supports native structures and an extensible plugin system. Valkey is compatible with various operating systems and architectures. It is a fork of the open-source Redis project.

Redisson is a Java client and real-time data platform for Valkey and Redis, providing additional services and data structures. It offers a range of features including distributed Java collections, objects, and service implementations. Redisson is compatible with JDK 1.8+ and the latest versions of Redis and Valkey. It also supports Android and provides a thread-safe implementation.

A Python ProxyPool for web spider, providing a pool of proxy IPs for crawling. It utilizes Redis for storage and handles HTTP requests. The project is designed to facilitate web scraping by managing proxy IPs. It is built using Python.

A GUI application for Redis, formerly known as RedisDesktopManager, now joining forces with Redis to enhance developer experience and productivity. It provides a graphical interface to interact with Redis. The project is now part of the Redis ecosystem. The application aims to simplify Redis development.

This project analyzes and explores the underlying implementation principles of mainstream technologies in the internet industry from a source code perspective, aiming to help developers improve their technical depth. It currently covers Spring, Mybatis, Netty, Dubbo, Redis, and Tomcat.
A Redis client for the Go programming language, providing an interface to interact with Redis databases.
Node-Redis is a modern, high performance Redis client for Node.js, built with TypeScript.

A distributed agile development system architecture based on Spring, SpringMVC, and Mybatis, providing a set of public microservice modules, including permission management, content management, and user management. It supports service governance, monitoring, and tracking, aiming to provide a comprehensive J2EE enterprise-level development solution for small and medium-sized enterprises.
key_features: ["Permission management", "Content management", "User management", "Payment center", "Storage system", "Configuration center", "Log analysis", "Task and notification management"]

summary: A robust, performance-focused, and full-featured Redis client for Node.js, built with TypeScript.
JuiceFS is a distributed POSIX file system built on top of Redis and S3, designed for big data and cloud-native applications.

Redis Python client, providing a Python interface to the Redis key-value store. It allows for interaction with Redis, including Redis Cluster. The client is designed for use with Python applications. It provides an interface to Redis, enabling data storage and retrieval.

Asynq is a Go library for queueing tasks and processing them asynchronously with workers, designed to be scalable and easy to get started. It's backed by Redis and provides a simple, reliable, and efficient distributed task queue. Asynq allows clients to put tasks on a queue and process them asynchronously with workers. It is suitable for background jobs and asynchronous tasks.

Codis is a proxy-based high performance Redis cluster solution written in Go, supporting pipeline and dynamic scaling. It is production-ready and widely used in many companies. Codis provides features like resharding without restarting the cluster and pipeline. It is an alternative to Twemproxy and Redis Cluster.
key_features: ["Proxy-based Redis cluster", "Dynamic scaling", "Pipeline", "Resharding without restarting cluster"]

summary: Tiny RDM is a lightweight Redis GUI client for Mac, Windows, and Linux, with a web version deployable via Docker. It is built using Vue and provides a modern, colorful interface. The project supports multiple platforms and has a web version. It is a Redis desktop manager with a simple and intuitive design.
KeyDB is a multithreaded fork of Redis, providing major improvements and consolidating Open Source and Enterprise offerings into a single project.

Jedis is a high-performance Redis Java client designed for ease of use, providing a simple and efficient way to interact with Redis. It is suitable for applications that require a reliable and efficient Redis connection. Jedis supports various Redis features, including Redis Cluster. For object mapping, it recommends using redis-om-spring.
twemproxy is a fast and lightweight proxy for memcached and redis protocols, designed to reduce connections to caching servers and enable horizontal scaling of distributed caching architecture.

Noodle is a productivity tool designed for students, aiming to provide a singular app for all their needs. It is currently in active development towards a minimal viable product (MVP). The project is a work-in-progress and not yet finished. Its purpose is to help students stay on track with their studies.

A community-driven framework for managing zsh configuration, including plugins and themes. It provides an auto-update tool for easy updates. The project has a large collection of plugins and themes for various technologies. It aims to enhance productivity in the terminal.

Uptime Kuma is a self-hosted monitoring tool built with JavaScript, providing an easy-to-use interface for monitoring uptime. It is a single-page app with a responsive design. The tool utilizes WebSockets for real-time updates and is designed to be self-hosted. It offers a simple and intuitive way to monitor uptime.

A repository containing questions and exercises on various technical topics related to DevOps and SRE, with a focus on preparing for interviews. It covers a wide range of technologies and concepts, including Linux, Jenkins, AWS, and Kubernetes. The repository has 2624 exercises and questions, and users can contribute by submitting pull requests. It's a useful resource for those pursuing a career as a DevOps engineer.
key_features: ["Interview questions and exercises", "DevOps and SRE topics", "Multi-cloud support"]

summary: The Moby Project is a collaborative project for the container ecosystem to assemble container-based systems. It provides a toolkit for assembling custom container-based systems and a community for experimentation and exchange. Moby is an open-source project created by Docker to enable and accelerate software containerization.

Traefik is a cloud native application proxy that enables users to easily manage and configure their application's traffic. It supports various technologies such as Docker, Kubernetes, and Marathon. Traefik provides features like load balancing, reverse proxy, and SSL termination. It is designed to work seamlessly with microservices architectures.

An unofficial Bitwarden compatible server written in Rust, providing an alternative to the official Bitwarden server for self-hosted deployment. It is compatible with official Bitwarden clients and is designed to be more resource-efficient. The project is formerly known as bitwarden_rs. It allows users to host their own Bitwarden server.

TrendRadar is an AI-driven public opinion and trend monitor that aggregates multi-platform data, RSS, and smart alerts. It supports keyword filtering, AI-powered news filtering, translation, and analysis. The project also integrates with various messaging platforms for smart push notifications.
key_features: ["Multi-platform aggregation", "RSS support", "Smart alerts", "Keyword filtering", "AI-powered news filtering", "AI translation", "AI analysis", "Smart push notifications"]

summary: A tool for exploring each layer in a Docker image, analyzing its contents, and discovering ways to shrink its size.
Run macOS VM in a Docker container with near-native performance, X11 Forwarding, and support for security research. It allows conducting security research on macOS using both Linux and Windows.
key_features: ["Mac OS X in Docker", "X11 Forwarding", "Near-native performance"]

summary: This project provides a Windows environment inside a Docker container, utilizing Docker and virtualization technologies. It features an ISO downloader, KVM acceleration, and a web-based viewer. The project can be run via Docker Compose and supports various configurations.

Lazydocker is a tool to manage Docker containers and images. It provides a simple and efficient way to manage everything Docker. The project is built using Go and aims to simplify Docker management. It is available for MacOS and Linux.

Mastodon is a self-hosted, globally interconnected microblogging community. It is a free, open-source social network server based on ActivityPub. The platform allows users to host their own social network server and connect with other servers. Mastodon provides a decentralized social networking experience.
A collection of Docker Compose samples for personal use, including single service samples and basic setups for different platforms.
Data Engineering Zoomcamp is a free 9-week course on building production-ready data pipelines, focusing on data engineering fundamentals and industry-standard tools.

Sunshine is a self-hosted game stream host for Moonlight, allowing users to stream games remotely. It is designed to work with various package managers and has a focus on game streaming. The project is built using C++ and utilizes Docker. It aims to provide a seamless game streaming experience for users.

Portainer is a lightweight service delivery platform for containerized applications, making Docker and Kubernetes management easy. It provides a simple GUI and API to manage orchestrator resources. Portainer supports deployment on any cluster as a Linux or Windows container. It has two editions: Community Edition and Business Edition.
key_features: ["Container management", "Orchestrator resource management", "GUI and API interface"]

summary: Docker Compose is a tool for defining and running multi-container applications with Docker. It allows users to define and run applications with multiple containers. The project is built using Go and provides a simple way to manage and orchestrate containers. It supports various operating systems, including Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Harness Open Source is an end-to-end developer platform with Source Control Management, CI/CD Pipelines, Hosted Developer Environments, and Artifact Registries. It provides automated DevOps pipelines, code hosting, and artifact registries. The platform is designed to streamline development and deployment processes. It is built using Go and utilizes Docker.
key_features: ["Source Control Management", "CI/CD Pipelines", "Hosted Developer Environments", "Artifact Registries"]

summary: Trivy is a comprehensive security scanner that detects vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, secrets, and SBOM in various targets such as containers, Kubernetes, code repositories, and clouds. It supports multiple programming languages, operating systems, and platforms. Trivy has scanners for OS packages, software dependencies, known vulnerabilities, IaC issues, and sensitive information. It is designed to be versatile and can scan different types of targets.
A curated list of Docker resources and projects, focusing on Docker integration and related tools.

1Panel is a modern, open-source VPS control panel with native AI agent support, allowing users to manage their entire server stack from a clean web interface. It supports Ollama models and OpenClaw agents. The project is trusted by over 2 million self-hosters worldwide. It provides a unified interface for server management.
key_features: ["VPS control panel", "AI agent support", "Server stack management", "Web interface"]

summary: CasaOS is a simple, easy-to-use, elegant open-source Personal Cloud system. It aims to provide autonomy, reduce SaaS costs, and maximize personalization. CasaOS is designed for home automation and self-hosted use cases. It offers a personalized copilot experience.

Glance is a self-hosted dashboard that aggregates various feeds into a single, customizable interface. It supports multiple widget types, including RSS feeds, social media, weather, and more. The dashboard is highly customizable and can be tailored to individual preferences. It is designed to be lightweight and streamlined.

AstrBot is an AI Agent Assistant and development framework that integrates multiple IM platforms, LLMs, plugins, and AI features. It can be used as an alternative to openclaw. The project utilizes AI and chatbot technologies to provide a comprehensive framework. It supports various platforms such as Discord, Telegram, and QQ.
key_features: ["IM platform integration", "LLM integration", "plugin support", "AI feature integration"]

summary: One-api is an LLM API management and redistribution system that unifies multiple providers under a single API, supporting various models such as OpenAI, Azure, and Google Gemini. It provides a single binary, Docker-ready, and has an English UI. The system enables key management and secondary distribution. It is designed to be used out of the box, with a standardized API format.

PDFMathTranslate is a project that translates PDF scientific papers with preserved formats using AI. It supports multiple translation services, including Google, DeepL, Ollama, and OpenAI. The project provides various interfaces, including CLI, GUI, MCP, Docker, and Zotero. It is available for multiple languages and platforms.

Chatwoot is an open-source live-chat, email support, and omni-channel desk platform, serving as an alternative to Intercom, Zendesk, and Salesforce Service Cloud. It provides a modern customer support solution. The platform is designed to handle multiple channels and offers a range of features for efficient customer support. It is built using Ruby and incorporates various technologies for a seamless experience.
A Docker container for managing Nginx proxy hosts with a simple interface, providing easy forwarding to websites with free SSL.

Podman is a tool for managing OCI containers and pods, providing an alternative to Docker. It allows users to run containers and pods without requiring a daemon. Podman supports Linux and OCI containers. It is designed to be compatible with Docker.

Dokku is a docker-powered PaaS that helps build and manage application lifecycles. It provides a self-hosted platform for deploying and managing applications. Dokku is similar to Heroku but self-hosted. It supports various deployment options including buildpacks and containers.
Colima is a container runtime for macOS and Linux with minimal setup, providing a simple CLI interface and support for Intel and Apple Silicon macOS, and Linux.
key_features: ["Simple CLI interface", "Support for Intel and Apple Silicon macOS, and Linux"]

summary: Harbor is an open source, cloud native registry project that stores, signs, and scans content. It is designed for container management and registry. The project utilizes a cloud-native approach and supports various container-related technologies. Harbor aims to provide a trusted and secure environment for container management.

Proxmox VE Helper-Scripts is a community-driven project providing one-command installations for services, containers, and VMs on Proxmox VE. It aims to simplify the setup process for various services and tools. The project is built on top of Proxmox VE and utilizes shell scripts. The goal is to make it easier for users to deploy and manage their homelab setups.

Kubernetes is an open source system for managing containerized applications across multiple hosts, providing deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications. It builds upon experience at Google running production workloads at scale. Kubernetes is hosted by the Cloud Native Computing Foundation. It provides basic mechanisms for the deployment, maintenance, and scaling of applications.

MinIO is a high-performance, S3 compatible object store. It is designed for speed and scalability, powering AI/ML, analytics, and data-intensive workloads. MinIO is no longer maintained, with alternatives available. It was released under the GNU AGPL v3.0 license.

etcd is a distributed reliable key-value store for the most critical data of a distributed system. It is designed to provide a highly available and fault-tolerant storage solution. etcd is used in distributed systems and is a key component of Kubernetes. It provides a reliable way to store and manage data in a distributed environment.

A tutorial on setting up Kubernetes from scratch, optimized for learning and understanding each task required to bootstrap a cluster. The results are not production-ready and may receive limited community support. The guide is intended for learning purposes, taking a detailed approach to ensure comprehension. It is not suitable for those seeking a fully automated tool.

Kong is an API and AI gateway that serves as a central entry point for managing APIs and microservices. It is designed to be cloud-native and supports Kubernetes and serverless architectures. Kong provides features such as API management, authentication, and caching. It is built using Lua and supports various technologies such as OpenAI proxy and reverse proxy.

Istio is an open source service mesh that connects, secures, controls, and observes services. It provides a transparent layer for managing microservices. Istio supports features like circuit breakers, fault injection, and request routing. It integrates with platforms like Kubernetes and Consul.

K9s is a Kubernetes CLI that provides a terminal UI to interact with Kubernetes clusters, making it easier to navigate, observe, and manage applications. It continually watches Kubernetes for changes and offers subsequent commands to interact with observed resources. The project aims to simplify the Kubernetes management experience. K9s is an open-source project maintained by its creator.

k3s is a lightweight version of Kubernetes, designed to be more resource-efficient and easier to use. It is built using Go and is compatible with Kubernetes (k8s). The project aims to provide a more accessible and streamlined Kubernetes experience. k3s is suitable for resource-constrained environments and edge computing.

SeaweedFS is a distributed storage system designed to handle billions of files with O(1) disk access and effortless horizontal scaling. It supports object storage, file systems, and Iceberg tables. SeaweedFS is built for scalability and high performance. It provides a reliable and efficient storage solution for large-scale applications.

Nacos is a dynamic service discovery, configuration, and service management platform for building AI cloud-native applications. It provides an easy-to-use platform for managing distributed configurations and services. Nacos supports various technologies such as Dubbo, Istio, and Kubernetes. It is designed for microservices architecture and cloud-native applications.

Minikube is a tool for running Kubernetes locally. It allows users to develop, test, and demonstrate Kubernetes applications on their local machine. Minikube supports a variety of container runtimes and can be used with a range of Kubernetes versions. It provides a convenient way to get started with Kubernetes development.

Consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure. It provides features such as service discovery and configuration for applications. Consul is designed to work with dynamic infrastructure and supports multiple data centers. It integrates with various technologies such as Kubernetes and ECS.

Helm is a tool for managing Charts, which are packages of pre-configured Kubernetes resources. It allows users to find and use popular software packaged as Helm Charts. Helm is used for managing Kubernetes resources. It is a package manager for Kubernetes.

A structured learning map for DevOps, covering principles, processes, tooling, and use cases. The project started as a personal learning journey and has become a community resource. It covers a range of topics related to DevOps. The project aims to provide a foundational knowledge of DevOps.

Authelia is an OpenID Certified Single Sign-On Multi-Factor portal for web apps, providing secure authentication and authorization. It supports various authentication methods, including 2FA, MFA, and OAuth2. The project is built using Go and can be deployed using Docker and Kubernetes. Authelia also supports LDAP and other security features.

OpenFaaS is a serverless functions framework that makes it easy for developers to deploy event-driven functions and microservices to Kubernetes. It allows packaging code or existing binaries in OCI-compatible images for highly scalable endpoints with auto-scaling and metrics.

A project to learn and understand Docker and container technologies with real DevOps practice. It covers the core concepts, principles, and practical skills of Docker. The project aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of Docker from scratch. It is based on real-world cases and best practices.

Dapr is a portable runtime for building distributed applications across cloud and edge, combining event-driven architecture with workflow orchestration. It enables durable execution, AI agents, and secure distributed applications. Dapr supports various technologies such as containers, Kubernetes, and serverless architecture. It provides a robust framework for building microservices-based applications.

Rancher is an open source container management platform built for organizations that deploy containers in production. It makes it easy to run Kubernetes everywhere, meet IT requirements, and empower DevOps teams. The platform provides a complete container management solution. It is built using Go and supports Docker and Kubernetes.

Pulumi is an Infrastructure as Code platform that allows users to manage and provision cloud infrastructure using any programming language. It supports major cloud providers such as AWS, Azure, and GCP. Pulumi also supports containers, Kubernetes, and serverless computing. It provides a flexible and scalable way to manage infrastructure.
key_features: ["Infrastructure as Code", "Multi-language support", "Cloud provider support", "Container support", "Kubernetes support", "Serverless support"]

summary: Kuboard is a free management interface for Kubernetes, providing features such as multi-cluster management, permission management, monitoring, and logging. It is compatible with Kubernetes version 1.13 and above. Kuboard has been widely adopted by over 1000 enterprises and has gained 10000+ GitHub stars.

Cilium is an eBPF-based networking, security, and observability solution for Kubernetes. It provides a simple flat Layer 3 network and enforces network policies on L3-L7 using an identity-based security model. Cilium also implements distributed load balancing and can replace kube-proxy. It is designed to work with containers and Kubernetes.

A cross-platform .NET sample microservices and container-based application that runs on Linux, Windows, and macOS. It is powered by .NET 7, Docker Containers, and Azure Kubernetes Services. The project supports various development environments, including Visual Studio, VS for Mac, and CLI-based environments. The project has been moved to a new repository for active development.

Argo CD is a declarative continuous deployment tool for Kubernetes, providing a simple and efficient way to manage and deploy applications. It supports various tools and technologies such as GitOps, Helm, and Kustomize. Argo CD automates the deployment of applications to Kubernetes, making it easier to manage complex deployments. It is designed to work seamlessly with Kubernetes and other DevOps tools.

Lens is a Kubernetes dashboard that provides a user-friendly interface for managing cloud-native applications and containers. It is designed to simplify the process of running Kubernetes. Lens offers a range of features to support devops and Kubernetes management. It provides a comprehensive platform for managing Kubernetes resources.

Authentik is an authentication solution that provides a glue for various authentication protocols. It supports OAuth2, OIDC, SAML, and other authentication standards. The project aims to simplify authentication and authorization for applications. It is designed to work with Kubernetes and provides a reverse proxy for secure authentication.

This project provides best practices on recommendation systems, utilizing Python and focusing on AI, machine learning, and data science. It includes tutorials and operationalization techniques. The project leverages Jupyter Notebook and Kubernetes for deployment. The goal is to create effective recommendation algorithms and engines.
key_features: ["Recommendation algorithms", "Rating and ranking systems", "Tutorial and operationalization"]

summary: containerd is an open and reliable container runtime. It is designed to provide a robust and efficient way to manage containers. The project is written in Go and is related to Kubernetes and Docker. It is a part of the Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF).

Teleport provides a secure way to access and protect infrastructure, offering features such as single sign-on, access controls, and audit capabilities. It supports various protocols, including SSH, Kubernetes, and RDP. Teleport enables secure tunnels and enforces Role-Based and Attribute-Based Access Control. It works with multiple resources, including servers, databases, and cloud APIs.

A cloud-first microservices demo application showcasing a web-based e-commerce app with 10 microservices. It demonstrates how to modernize enterprise applications using Google Cloud products. The application allows users to browse items, add them to the cart, and purchase them. It is used by Google to showcase the use of Google Cloud products.
kubectx is a tool to switch between contexts (clusters) on kubectl faster, and launch readonly shells for each context. It also includes kubens, a tool to switch between Kubernetes namespaces easily.

A step-by-step guide on how to become a DevOps engineer, with links to relevant learning resources. The roadmap provides a comprehensive overview of the DevOps landscape, helping learners navigate the field and make informed decisions about what to learn next.

Ingress NGINX Controller for Kubernetes, providing ingress control functionality. The project is set to retire in March 2026. Existing deployments will not be broken, but no further releases or updates will be made. The project utilizes Go as its primary programming language.

gVisor is an Application Kernel for Containers, running in userspace. It provides a kernel-like environment for containers, leveraging technologies like Docker and Kubernetes. The project focuses on sandboxing and security. It is designed to work with Linux and OCI standards.

TensorFlow is an open-source machine learning framework for everyone, providing a wide range of tools and libraries for deep learning and neural networks. It supports distributed training and has a large community of contributors. TensorFlow is primarily used for building and training machine learning models. It has a wide range of applications, including computer vision, natural language processing, and robotics.

This repository provides models and examples built with TensorFlow, demonstrating best practices for modeling and aiming to improve transparency and reproducibility. It includes training logs on TensorBoard.dev for certain models. The project is designed for TensorFlow users to take full advantage of the framework for research and product development. The repository offers a collection of modeling solutions.

Keras is a multi-backend deep learning framework that supports JAX, TensorFlow, PyTorch, and OpenVINO. It allows for effortless building and training of models for various applications, including computer vision, natural language processing, and more. Keras provides accelerated model development and state-of-the-art performance. It also enables datacenter-scale training, making it suitable for large-scale deployments.
concepts: ["Deep Learning", "Machine Learning", "Neural Networks", "Computer Vision", "Natural Language Processing"]

summary: This project implements real-time voice cloning using transfer learning from speaker verification to multispeaker text-to-speech synthesis. It utilizes a vocoder for real-time functionality and was developed as a master's thesis. The project enables cloning a voice in 5 seconds to generate arbitrary speech in real-time. It leverages deep learning techniques for voice cloning.
A repository providing reusable computer vision tools, utilizing deep learning and machine learning techniques for image and video processing.
A TensorFlow tutorial and examples for beginners, supporting both TF v1 and v2, with a focus on deep learning and machine learning.
key_features: ["Notebooks with explanation", "Source codes", "Traditional 'raw' TensorFlow implementations", "Latest TensorFlow API practices"]

summary: Ray is an AI compute engine that consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads. It supports various machine learning tasks, including deep learning, reinforcement learning, and hyperparameter optimization. Ray is designed for large-scale AI applications and provides a scalable and flexible platform for AI development. It integrates with popular libraries like PyTorch and TensorFlow.

BERT is a TensorFlow project that provides code and pre-trained models for natural language processing tasks. It includes smaller BERT models for environments with restricted computational resources. The models can be fine-tuned and are effective in knowledge distillation. The goal is to enable research in institutions with fewer computational resources.

PhotoPrism is an AI-powered app for privately browsing, organizing, and sharing photos and videos. It uses the latest technologies to tag and find pictures. The app is self-hosted and can be used on premises or in a private cloud.
key_features: ["Photo and video browsing", "Organizing and sharing", "AI-powered tagging"]

summary: Frigate is a complete and local NVR designed for Home Assistant with AI object detection. It uses OpenCV and Tensorflow to perform realtime object detection locally for IP cameras. A GPU or AI accelerator is highly recommended for optimal performance. It has tight integration with Home Assistant.
key_features: ["Realtime object detection", "Local NVR", "IP camera support", "Home Assistant integration"]

summary: Netron is a visualizer for neural network, deep learning, and machine learning models. It supports various formats such as ONNX, TensorFlow Lite, and PyTorch. The project allows users to view and explore machine learning models. Netron has experimental support for several other frameworks.

This repository contains a collection of data science Python notebooks, covering topics such as deep learning, big data, and machine learning. It utilizes various libraries and frameworks, including TensorFlow, Keras, and scikit-learn. The notebooks also explore data analysis and visualization using matplotlib, pandas, and NumPy. Additionally, it includes examples of working with AWS and command lines.
key_features: ["Deep learning implementations", "Big data processing", "Data analysis and visualization", "Machine learning examples", "AWS integration"]

summary: An interactive deep learning book with multi-framework code, math, and discussions, adopted at 500 universities worldwide. It covers various topics including computer vision, natural language processing, and reinforcement learning. The book provides a hands-on approach to learning deep learning concepts. It is used in courses such as STAT 157 at UC Berkeley.
concepts: ["Deep Learning", "Machine Learning", "Computer Vision", "Natural Language Processing", "Reinforcement Learning"]

summary: Spleeter is a Deezer source separation library that includes pretrained models for audio processing tasks. It utilizes deep learning techniques to separate audio into different components such as vocals, drums, and bass. The library is built using Python and TensorFlow. It provides a powerful tool for music and audio processing applications.

DeepSpeech is an open source embedded speech-to-text engine that can run in real time on various devices. It uses machine learning techniques based on Baidu's Deep Speech research paper. The project is now discontinued. It supports offline and on-device speech recognition.

This project teaches the fundamentals of Machine Learning in Python, containing example code and solutions to exercises from the book Hands-on Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn and TensorFlow. It is now deprecated and has been replaced by newer versions. The project utilizes Jupyter Notebooks for its implementation. It focuses on deep learning, neural networks, and machine learning concepts.

Mask R-CNN is an implementation for object detection and instance segmentation using Keras and TensorFlow. It includes source code, training code, pre-trained weights, and evaluation metrics. The code is designed to be easy to extend and is documented for research purposes.

A ranked list of awesome machine learning Python libraries, updated weekly. It covers various topics such as automl, data analysis, data science, and deep learning. The list includes popular libraries like Keras, PyTorch, and scikit-learn. It is a valuable resource for machine learning enthusiasts and professionals.
key_features: ["Ranked list of machine learning libraries", "Weekly updates"]

summary: ncnn is a high-performance neural network inference framework optimized for mobile, embedded, and desktop deployment. It supports CPU and Vulkan GPU backends and provides tools for converting models from PyTorch and ONNX. ncnn is used in various Tencent applications, including QQ, Qzone, WeChat, and Pitu.
key_features: ["Neural Network Inference", "Model Conversion", "CPU and GPU Support"]

summary: A 100-day machine learning project with tutorials and implementations in Chinese. It covers supervised and unsupervised learning, including data preprocessing, linear regression, logistic regression, and more.
This repository provides code, exercises, and solutions for popular Reinforcement Learning algorithms, serving as a learning tool to complement theoretical materials.

The largest hub of ready-to-use datasets for AI models with fast, easy-to-use and efficient data manipulation tools. It provides a wide range of datasets for various AI applications. The project aims to make data manipulation easy and efficient. It supports multiple AI models and datasets.

The ONNX project is an open standard for machine learning interoperability, allowing models to be transferred between different frameworks and platforms. It supports various machine learning frameworks such as Keras, PyTorch, and TensorFlow. The project enables seamless integration and deployment of machine learning models across different environments. It aims to provide a common format for machine learning models, facilitating collaboration and innovation in the field.

ONNX Runtime is a cross-platform, high performance ML inferencing and training accelerator that supports models from deep learning frameworks and classical machine learning libraries, providing optimal performance by leveraging hardware accelerators and graph optimizations.

Screenity is a free and privacy-friendly screen recorder with no limits. It is built using JavaScript and provides features such as annotation, audio, and video recording. The project is sponsored by Recall.ai, an API for desktop recording. Screenity is a powerful tool for screen capture and screencasting.

JavaScript API for face detection and face recognition in the browser and nodejs with tensorflow.js. It provides functionalities such as age estimation, emotion recognition, face detection, face landmarks, face recognition, and gender recognition. The API is implemented on top of tensorflow.js core and has live demos and tutorials available. It can be used for realtime face tracking and recognition.
key_features: ["Face Detection", "Face Recognition", "Age Estimation", "Emotion Recognition", "Face Landmarks", "Gender Recognition"]

summary: A deep learning tutorial repository providing learning resources and materials for beginners, covering topics such as CNN, GAN, and machine learning. It includes a curated list of articles, tutorials, and courses. The repository utilizes Jupyter Notebook for its tech stack. The project aims to provide a comprehensive learning path for AI and deep learning.
A curated list of dedicated resources for TensorFlow, including models, projects, libraries, tools, and tutorials.
This repository provides simple and ready-to-use tutorials for TensorFlow, including source code and documentation for deep learning.

CVAT is a platform for building high-quality visual datasets for vision AI, offering open-source, cloud, and enterprise products for image, video, and 3D annotation with AI-assisted labeling, quality assurance, team collaboration, analytics, and developer APIs. It provides a range of tools and services for computer vision annotation. CVAT supports various annotation tasks, including object detection, semantic segmentation, and image classification. It also offers features like team collaboration, analytics, and developer APIs.

Kubeflow is a machine learning toolkit for Kubernetes, providing a foundation of tools for AI platforms. It is composable, modular, portable, and scalable, covering every stage of the AI lifecycle. Kubeflow offers modular, scalable, and extensible tools to support AI use cases. It is designed for AI practitioners, platform administrators, and developers.

A Natural Language Processing tutorial for deep learning researchers, focusing on PyTorch and implementing various NLP models with minimal code. The tutorial covers basic embedding models, neural network language models, and other topics. It is designed for beginners and uses PyTorch version 1.0 or higher. The tutorial also includes examples and papers for further learning.
key_features: ["Basic Embedding Model", "Neural Network Language Model", "Predict Next Word"]

summary: This repository provides State-of-the-Art Deep Learning examples that are easy to train and deploy, achieving the best reproducible accuracy and performance with NVIDIA CUDA-X software stack running on NVIDIA Volta, Turing and Ampere GPUs. The examples are organized by models and are suitable for various applications such as computer vision, natural language processing, and speech recognition. The repository uses Jupyter Notebook and supports multiple deep learning frameworks. The examples are also available in a monthly updated Docker container on the NVIDIA GPU Cloud (NGC) container registry.

summary: This repository hosts pre-trained TensorFlow.js models that can be used in any project. The models are hosted on NPM and unpkg, and can be used directly or in a transfer learning setting. The API is designed to be used by non-machine learning experts. The repository aims to provide a set of models that complement each other and can be used as building blocks in other apps.

Virgilio is a mentor for Data Science E-Learning, providing a guided path for studying data science and related topics. It utilizes a GenAI Framework for robust and easy-to-learn functionality. The project aims to simplify the process of studying and reading through the vast amount of information available on the internet. It focuses on innovative fields such as data science, machine learning, and computer vision.

Horovod is a distributed training framework for deep learning models. It supports TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch, and Apache MXNet. The framework allows for scalable and efficient training of models. It is designed for use with various technologies such as MPI and Ray.

This repository contains the official TensorFlow implementation of a style-based generator architecture for generative adversarial networks, allowing control over different aspects of generated images.

An open source AutoML toolkit for automating the machine learning lifecycle, including feature engineering, neural architecture search, model compression, and hyper-parameter tuning. It supports various machine learning algorithms and frameworks. The project utilizes Python as its primary programming language. It aims to simplify the machine learning process.

summary: Face recognition using Tensorflow, implementing ideas from FaceNet and Deep Face Recognition papers. The project utilizes computer vision and deep learning techniques for face detection and recognition.

Deeplearning4j is a suite of tools for deploying and training deep learning models using the JVM, with features including model import and a modular math library. It supports various frameworks and languages, including Java, Python, and Scala. The project aims to support all the needs of a JVM-based deep learning application. It also includes tools for linear algebra and neural networks.

summary: Ivy is a project that converts machine learning code between frameworks. It supports frameworks such as PyTorch and TensorFlow. The project utilizes Python as its primary programming language. Ivy aims to simplify the process of transitioning between different machine learning frameworks.

Open3D is a modern library for 3D data processing, providing a comprehensive set of tools for tasks such as 3D perception, computer graphics, and machine learning. It supports various technologies including CUDA, GPU, and OpenGL. The library is designed to be efficient and scalable, making it suitable for a wide range of applications. Open3D is implemented in C++ and also provides Python bindings.

A series of Jupyter notebooks that walk you through the fundamentals of Machine Learning and Deep Learning in Python using Scikit-Learn, Keras and TensorFlow 2. This project aims at teaching you the fundamentals of Machine Learning in python. It contains the example code and solutions to the exercises in the third edition of the O'Reilly book Hands-on Machine Learning with Scikit-Learn, Keras and TensorFlow.

summary: An open source deep learning book based on TensorFlow 2.0, providing a combination of theoretical and practical knowledge for beginners.

Ai-Learn is a project that provides a learning roadmap for artificial intelligence, including over 200 practical cases and projects, as well as free accompanying textbooks. It covers various topics such as Python, mathematics, machine learning, data analysis, and deep learning. The project aims to help students quickly start their AI self-study plan and begin working on practical projects efficiently.

summary: A collection of machine learning tutorials, including topics such as neural networks, numpy, and sklearn. The project uses Python as its primary programming language. It covers various machine learning concepts and techniques, including evolutionary algorithms. The tutorials are designed to help users learn and implement machine learning models.

Stable Diffusion web UI is a web interface for Stable Diffusion, implemented using the Gradio library. It provides features such as outpainting, inpainting, and image generation. The interface allows users to interact with the Stable Diffusion model, including specifying attention to certain parts of the text. The project utilizes deep learning and image processing techniques to generate images.

Transformers is a model-definition framework for state-of-the-art machine learning models in text, vision, audio, and multimodal models. It supports both inference and training. The framework is designed to work with various models, including pretrained models. It is primarily used for natural language processing and deep learning tasks.
ComfyUI is a powerful and modular diffusion model GUI, API, and backend with a graph/nodes interface, designed for content creation using AI.

PyTorch is a Python package providing tensor computation and deep neural networks with strong GPU acceleration. It features a tape-based autograd system and allows reuse of popular Python packages like NumPy, SciPy, and Cython. PyTorch supports continuous integration and has a healthy trunk. It is primarily used for deep learning and machine learning tasks.

This repository contains the code for developing, pretraining, and finetuning a GPT-like Large Language Model from scratch in PyTorch. It provides a step-by-step guide to building a large language model, mirroring the approach used in creating large-scale foundational models. The project focuses on educational purposes, allowing users to learn and understand how large language models work from the inside out. The repository is also the official code repository for the book Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch).

summary: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for Large Language Models (LLMs). It provides easy, fast, and cheap LLM serving for everyone. The project utilizes a range of technologies including PyTorch and CUDA. vLLM aims to make LLM serving accessible.

This repository contains 60+ implementations and tutorials of deep learning papers with side-by-side notes, covering topics such as transformers, optimizers, GANs, and reinforcement learning. The project aims to provide simple PyTorch implementations of neural networks and related algorithms, documented with explanations. The repository is actively maintained with new implementations added almost weekly. It helps users understand deep learning algorithms better through side-by-side formatted notes.

summary: Ultralytics YOLO is a project that utilizes deep learning and computer vision for object detection, image classification, and other related tasks. It is built using Python and PyTorch. The project provides various functionalities such as instance segmentation, pose estimation, and tracking. It is a part of the YOLO family of models.

Ultralytics YOLOv5 is a real-time object detection system implemented in PyTorch and deployable to various platforms including ONNX, CoreML, and TFLite. It utilizes deep learning and machine learning concepts for object detection tasks. The project supports multiple languages and has various applications in areas such as iOS. The system is designed for efficient and accurate object detection.
